questions.stem:
SRE 6 Acs C M1-3 Writer Lydia Davis observed that while literary forms, such as the poem, are recognizable as such even as
they evolve, there are pathbreaking "intergenetic" forms, that might, for example, use elements of both fables and realist
narratives to create something unclassifiable, Davis's own very short literary pieces arguably fit in this category, since they
straddle the line between prose and poetry. Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

'Though John Crowley, author of Flint and Mirror, is perhaps not as well known as the most commercially successfull American
writers of the past fifty years, his work has had several influential champions, including the poet James Merrill and the literary
critic Harold Bloom. In his afterword to Crowley's book Little, Big, Bloom praises how the novel adroitly blends what playwright
Friedrich Schiller termed th and sentimental modes - while Schiller thought works could be classified as either naive (seeking to
describe reality) or sentimental (seeking to develop ideas), Little, Big fuses both modes of writing, Which choice best describes
the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.D: It contrasts the opinions of two critics.

alone does not guarantee that a probe will accomplish its mission. if it had been sent Dermatological researchers and

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The Future of Nostalgia is a scholarly book by literary

nostalgia interested in re-creating the past. + Chapter 5 explores the Soviet-style restaurant the Nostalgija SnackBar in
Ljubljana. In chapter 5, Boym writes, "The Nostalgija Snack Bar] is a new kind space that plays with the past and the present."
The student wants to provide a quotation from chapter 5. Which choice most cffe int information from the notes to a vely uses
rel :complish this goal? A. Svetlana Boym defines a form of nostalgia interested in re-creating the past in chapter 4 of her book.

choices.A: Astronomers renamed Planet X in 1930 to honor Lowell for his contributions to the discovery of Pluto.
choices.B:
After a new telescope was built at Lowell's observatory in 1929, Clyde Tombaugh recorded the first photographic evidence of
Plane X, later renamed Pluto.
choices.C: In 1930, Lowell became the first person to record photographic evidence of Pluto, known at the time as Planet X.
choices.D: Lowell began the search for Pluto in 1905 and later left money to upgrade the observatory where it was discovered in 1930.

0°C whereas sample F02 formed at 20.8°C. Which finding, if tue, would most directly weaken the researchers' conclusion? 'A.
At a depth of 140 meters in the nautilids' habitat, the water is 20.8°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal to that in
sample FO2. B, The concentration of oxygen-18 in FO2 is higher than that in M23. C. The concentration of oxygen-18 in F02 is
lower than that in M23. D. At a depth of 385 meters in the nautilids" ha er is 14.0°C and the concentration of oxygen-18 is equal
to that in sample M23. i, the In Ding (Navajo) culture, ikaah (sandpaintings) are created and then erased as part of sacred
healing ceremonies lasting no more than a few days, but Diné hataatié(chanter; healer; cultural guide) Fred Stevens developed
fixatives to preserve desacralized sandpaintings. While on a US-sponsored cultural ambassadorial trip in Europe and the
Americas in the 1960s, Stevens produced sal such sandpaintings and gave them to cultural institutions. This may seem in
tension with the role of cultural ambassador - how could static objects authentically represent an inherently ephemeral and
dynamic practice? - but such a view is itself overly object-focused and neglects how Stevens, strove to convey exactly those
characteristics of ikaah as a cultural practice. Which quotation from an art historian would most drectly support the claim made
in the text? 'A. "Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings are best understood not as self-contained objects but as reminders of
the public creations of the sandpaintings, during which Stevens conducted appropriate 'Aaah rituals and encouraged viewers to
closely track the sand throughout the process, B, "While Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings are undoubtedly educative for
his movements and subtle shift audiences that have little exposure to Diné culture, they should not be confused with authentic

ikaah, which cannot be extricated from a practice that is intentionally and necessarily transitory nor condensed into a single
persistent object." C. "The most compelling way to reconcile the apparent tension between the temporary and performative
nature of ikaah and the persistent and static nature of Stevens's ambassadorial sandpaintings is to recognize that Stevens was
an ambassador not only of Diné culture but of a US art culture that tended to value permanent works over ephemeral ones."
performative role - of the sandpaintings, for by taking those actions, Stevens publicly enacted the transmission of knowledge of
both traditional rkaah practices and cutting-edge chemistry (in the form of the preservative additives), a hybrid that It is
important to recall Stevens's own actions as a gift-giver - a temporary and reflects the dynamism of Diné culture.

colleagues studied how members of the Yupno, an Indigenous group in Papua New Guinea, conceptualize time in both spoken

Upper-atmospheric jets - phenomena whereby electrical discharges cause narrow cones of blue light to briefly burst upward
from the tops of thunderclouds - have been observed reaching the ionosphere. The extreme altitudes involved (the ionosphere
begins about 80 km above Earth) mark these gigantic jets as outliers; the majority of jets reach heights of only 20 to 50 km.
